LAW AND MOTION 301, FURTHER HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANT PRECISION ENGINEERING, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-DEFENDANT PRECISION ENGINEERING, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PRECISION SATISFIED ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN BY SHOWING THAT THE DISCOVERY RESPONSES OF CROSS-COMPLAINANT SHIMMICK CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. ARE DEVOID OF FACTS SHOWING THAT ANY CONDUCT OF PRECISION IS RESPONSIBLE FOR THE CONSTRUCTION DEFECTS COMPLAINED OF BY PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND SHIMMICK. BECAUSE SHIMMICK HAS NOT SUBMITTED ANY EVIDENCE ON THIS MOTION SHOWING THAT PRECISION IS RESPONSIBLE FOR THOSE CONSTRUCTION DEFECTS, PRECISION IS ENTITLED TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT IN ITS FAVOR. THIS RULING DOES NOT APPLY TO ANY CLAIM THAT SHIMMICK MAY HAVE THAT PRECISION BREACHED A CONTRACTUAL DUTY TO DEFEND SHIMMICK FOR ANY ASPECT OF THIS CASE UP TO OCTOBER 10, 2025. THAT CLAIM WAS NOT ALLEGED IN SHIMMICK'S CROSS-COMPLAINT AGAINST PRECISION OR ADDRESSED IN THE MOTION PAPERS FILED BY PRECISION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD E. Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies and adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT PRECISION ENGINEERING, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication By Cross-Defendant Precision Engineering, Inc.

Cross-defendant Precision Engineering, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Precision has satisfied its summary judgment burden by showing that the discovery responses of cross-complainant Shimmick Construction Company, Inc. are devoid of facts showing that any conduct of Precision is responsible for the construction defects complained of by plaintiff City and County of San Francisco and Shimmick. Because Shimmick has not submitted any evidence on this motion showing that Precision is responsible for those construction defects, Precision is entitled to summary judgment in its favor.

Court invokes the rule of the Lilienthal case and determines that there are separate claims within the pleaded causes of action in the cross-complaint filed by Shimmick Construction Company as to indemnity and defense. Court grants Summary Adjudication as to the indemnity claim in favor of Cross-Defendant Precision Engineering, Inc. for the reasons stated. Court sets further hearing and briefing schedule as to the defense claims as agreed to by parties present. Shimmick Construction Company's Supplemental Opposition papers to be filed and served by December 8, 2024. Cross-Defendant Precision Engineering, Inc's Supplemental Reply papers to be filed and served by December 17, 2025. Courtesy copies of the supplemental opposition and reply are to be provided on the following day of service. Hearing is set for December 23, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 301.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
